questions.question: What is the average of the following 5 consecutive even numbers: 37, 39, 41, 43, 45?
questions.trick: For a consecutive arithmetic sequence, the average is simply the exact middle term.

questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 4, 8, 16, 32, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows a Geometric Progression with a constant multiplier ratio of 2. So, 32 * 2 = 64.
questions.trick: If numbers scale up very quickly, look for multiplication rather than addition.

questions.question: In March 2024, two new Election Commissioners were appointed to the Election Commission of India. Who among the following is NOT one of them?
options: Both A and B are the new appointees
questions.explanation: Gyanesh Kumar and Sukhbir Singh Sandhu were appointed as Election Commissioners in March 2024. Rajiv Kumar is currently the Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) and was not a new appointee in March.
questions.trick: The question asks 'NOT one of them'. Rajiv Kumar is a prominent figure as the CEC, which might lead one to incorrectly select him as a new appointee if not careful. The other two are the recent appointees.

questions.question: If an article is bought for Rs. 250 and sold at a profit of 40%, what is the selling price?
questions.trick: Calculate 10% of CP, multiply, and add it conceptually.

questions.question: Find the odd one out among the following numbers: 65, 16, 9, 25
questions.explanation: 9, 16, and 25 are perfect squares. 65 is not a perfect square.
questions.trick: Memorize squares up to 30 to instantly spot discrepancies.

questions.question: When did the Indian Constitution come into effect?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India came into effect on Republic Day, Jan 26, 1950.
questions.trick: 26 Jan was chosen to honor the 'Purna Swaraj' declaration of 1930.

questions.question: In which year did Mahatma Gandhi return to India from South Africa?
questions.explanation: Gandhi returned to India on Jan 9, 1915 (Pravasi Bharatiya Divas).
questions.trick: Remember 1915 as the start of his Indian movements.

questions.question: Which vitamin is produced when skin is exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: UVB rays synthesize Vitamin D in the body.

questions.question: Which specific experiment was successfully conducted by ISRO on March 22, 2024, involving its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV)?
questions.explanation: ISRO successfully conducted the second RLV Landing Experiment (RLV LEX-02) at the Aeronautical Test Range (ATR), Chitradurga, Karnataka, on March 22, 2024. This demonstrated autonomous landing capabilities under more challenging conditions.
questions.trick: LEX stands for Landing EXperiment, and '02' signifies the second in this series. Options could distract with other possible phases of RLV testing.

questions.question: What will be the Simple Interest on a principal amount of Rs. 3800 at an interest rate of 7% per annum over 2 years?
In its first Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting of FY25 held in April 2024, what was the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) decision regarding the repo rate?
questions.explanation: The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee, in its meeting from April 3-5, 2024, decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the seventh consecutive time, while maintaining its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
questions.trick: The phrase 'withdrawal of accommodation' might suggest a rate hike or a change, but the actual decision was to hold rates steady due to persistent inflation concerns and a focus on growth.

questions.question: According to the Reporters Without Borders (RSF) World Press Freedom Index 2024, released in May 2024, what is India's rank out of 180 countries?
questions.explanation: India ranked 159th out of 180 countries in the World Press Freedom Index 2024, published by Reporters Without Borders (RSF) on May 3, 2024. This represents a slight improvement from 161st in 2023.
questions.trick: India's rank in such global indices often fluctuates and is a topic of discussion. Remembering the exact, most recent rank among closely placed numbers can be challenging.

questions.question: A train travels at an average speed of 107 km/hr. How much distance will it cover in 6 hours?
questions.trick: Basic multiplication rule: D = S * T.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: At about 6,650 km long, it flows into the Mediterranean Sea.
questions.trick: Amazon is largest by volume, Nile is longest by length.

questions.question: India's General Elections 2024 are being conducted in how many phases?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India announced that the 2024 Lok Sabha elections would be held in 7 phases, starting from April 19, 2024, with results declared on June 4, 2024.
questions.trick: Often, large elections in India have multiple phases; remembering the exact number can be tricky. Some might confuse it with assembly elections that typically have fewer phases.

questions.question: If CAT is coded as FDW, how will CUP be coded in that same language?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by exactly 3 positions in the English alphabet.
questions.trick: Note the position gap between the first letter of the word and the first letter of its code.

questions.question: Which neighbouring country's Prime Minister visited India in mid-March 2024, holding extensive talks with Indian leadership on bilateral cooperation?
questions.explanation: Bhutanese Prime Minister Tshering Tobgay paid an official visit to India from March 14-18, 2024, engaging in discussions with PM Narendra Modi and other Indian officials to deepen bilateral ties, especially regarding the Gelephu Mindfulness City project.
questions.trick: Many South Asian leaders visit India frequently. One might confuse it with a visit from another close neighbor without recalling the specific timeline.

questions.question:
Statements:
1. All Cars are Trucks.
2. All Trucks are Vehicles.

Which conclusion logically follows?
questions.explanation: If A is a subset of B, and B is a subset of C, then A must be a subset of C completely.
questions.trick: Draw a venn diagram: Small circle A inside Medium B inside Large C.
